Short Title of 1986 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–399, title IX, § 901100 Stat. 889Aug. 27, 1986“This title [enacting and sections 1801 to 1809 of the former Appendix to Title 46, Shipping] may be cited as the ‘International Maritime and Port Security Act’.”section 1226 of this title

Short Title of 1978 Amendment, , , provided:Pub. L. 95–474, § 192 Stat. 1471Oct. 17, 1978“That this Act [enacting sections 1225, 1228 to 1231, and 1232 of this title, amending this section, sections 1222 to 1224, 1226, and 1227 of this title, and sections 214 and 391a of Title 46, Shipping, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and and section 391a of former Title 46] may be cited as the ‘Port and Tanker Safety Act of 1978’.”section 1224 of this title

Short Title, , , as amended by , , , which provided that , enacting this chapter, could be cited as the “Ports and Waterways Safety Act”, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 92–340, § 186 Stat. 424Pub. Transitional and Savings ProvisionsFor transitional and savings provisions and treatment of references to former provisions of this chapter in other laws, see , set out as a note preceding , Shipping.section 403 of Pub. L. 115–282section 101 of Title 46

Separability, , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–474, § 6(c)92 Stat. 1493Oct. 17, 1978“If a provision of this Act [see Short Title of 1978 Amendment note above] or the application of such provision to any person or circumstances shall be held invalid, the remainder of the Act and the application of such provision to persons or circumstances other than those to which it is held invalid shall not be affected thereby.”
